summary: Portfolio website for Sulekha Sahoo, a Professional Graphic Designer. Site includes hero, about me, skills gallery, education timeline, masonry portfolio grid, service cards, contact form, and footer. An Admin Dashboard with secure login (Internet Identity) and one-click portfolio photo upload/replace functionality is being added, so Sulekha can manage portfolio images and content without touching code.
architectural_notes: Sky blue accent color palette with white space and minimalist-vibrant aesthetic
Smooth scroll animations throughout the site
Admin Dashboard protected by Internet Identity (passkey/Google/Apple auth); portfolio images stored in app backend for persistence

existing_features.title: Implement a visually striking Hero section with the bold headline 'Creative Design, Unique Ideas', a subheading that introduces Sulekha Sahoo as a Professional Graphic Designer, and a prominent CTA button labeled 'View My Work' that smooth-scrolls to the Portfolio section. The hero should feature a full-viewport-height layout with an eye-catching background treatment.

Implement a sticky top navigation bar with links: Home, Work, Education, and Contact. Each link smooth-scrolls to the corresponding section. The nav should remain visible on scroll and collapse into a hamburger menu on mobile.

Implement an About Me section displaying the bio: 'A creative graphics designer who loves color and helping brands look professional.' Also include her declaration: 'Designs that reflect creativity, skill, and an understanding of design principles.' Mention she is bilingual in Odia and Hindi. The section should use generous white space and a clean typographic layout.

Implement a Skills Gallery section showcasing expertise in Adobe Photoshop, Illustrator, CorelDRAW, and InDesign. Each skill should be represented with a clean icon (use well-known SVG icons or styled placeholder icons) and its label. Layout should be a responsive icon grid.

Implement an Education Timeline section displaying her academic journey in chronological order: Nimatpur Girls High School (BSE), CHSE (+2), +3 Degree, and specialized PGDCA/Multimedia training at Mayuri Multimedia. Use a vertical timeline UI with milestone markers and brief labels for each stage.

Implement a Service Cards section with three cards: 'Logo Design', 'Poster Design', and 'Social Media Management'. Each card should have a short descriptive tagline and a relevant icon or illustration. Cards should be displayed in a responsive 3-column grid (stacking on mobile).

Implement a Portfolio Grid section (labeled 'Work') with a 3-column masonry-style grid showcasing sample placeholder items for three categories: Logos, Posters, and Social Media Content. Each grid item should display a category label and a visually styled placeholder tile. Include category filter buttons (All, Logos, Posters, Social Media) that filter visible items.

Implement a Contact section with a simple contact form (fields: Name, Email, Message) and a Send button. Below or beside the form, display her contact details: email sulekhasaho09865@gmail.com and a phone number placeholder. The form should have basic client-side validation (non-empty fields, valid email format). No backend submission is required — display a success toast/message on submit.

Implement a Footer section displaying her address (Pasarbindha, Balasore), social media placeholder icons/links (Instagram, Behance, LinkedIn), and a copyright line. The footer should be clean and minimal.

Apply a cohesive visual theme across the entire site: minimalist white/off-white base, vibrant accent color using sky blue (#38BDF8 or similar), clean sans-serif typography with large bold headings. Add smooth scroll behavior site-wide and subtle scroll-triggered entrance animations (fade-in, slide-up) on section elements as they come into view.

Review the existing portfolio site and clarify what specific changes the user wants. In the meantime, make the following visible polish improvements: (1) Increase the hero section headline font size for greater visual impact on desktop. (2) Ensure all navigation links scroll precisely to their target sections without offset issues. (3) Add a real phone number placeholder '+91 XXXXX XXXXX' in the Contact section if not already present. (4) Make the portfolio grid items slightly taller for better visual balance. (5) Ensure the footer copyright year reads 2025.

In the About Me section (frontend/src/components/About.tsx), remove the floating '5+ years experience' badge entirely. Since Sulekha is a fresher with 0 years of experience, this badge should not appear on the page at all.

Add an Admin login page at route /admin (or toggled via a hidden URL) that uses the existing Internet Identity hook (useInternetIdentity) to authenticate. After successful login, redirect to the Admin Dashboard. Show a 'Login with Internet Identity' button and brief instruction text. The page must be unreachable from public navigation links.

Build an Admin Dashboard page (visible only when authenticated via Internet Identity) with: (1) a grid of current portfolio items fetched from the backend, each showing a thumbnail, title, category, and action buttons ('Replace Image', 'Delete'); (2) a 'Quick Upload' form with drag-and-drop or file picker for image files, a title input, a category selector (Branding, Social Media, Print Media), and a 'Publish' button that calls the backend addPortfolioItem; (3) a 'Logout' button. Replacing an image calls replacePortfolioItem. Deleting calls deletePortfolioItem. All mutations optimistically update the UI and show toast notifications on success or failure.

Update the public Portfolio section (frontend/src/components/Portfolio.tsx) to fetch live portfolio items from the backend using the getPortfolioItems query via useActor, replacing the current static placeholder data. Category filter buttons should be updated to 'All', 'Branding', 'Social Media', and 'Print Media' to match the new admin categories. If the backend returns no items, fall back to displaying a tasteful empty-state message ('No work uploaded yet — check back soon!').

In frontend/src/components/Footer.tsx, locate the second anchor link (<a>) inside the second paragraph of the footer's bottom bar (corresponding to XPath /html[1]/body[1]/div[1]/div[1]/footer[1]/div[1]/div[2]/p[2]/a[1]) and hide it completely by removing it from the rendered output or setting it to not render.

In frontend/src/components/Skills.tsx, remove the three <span> elements identified by the following XPaths from the skill proficiency cards rendered in section 4: (1) /html[1]/body[1]/div[1]/div[1]/main[1]/section[4]/div[1]/div[3]/div[2]/div[1]/div[1]/div[1]/div[1]/span[1], (2) /html[1]/body[1]/div[1]/div[1]/main[1]/section[4]/div[1]/div[3]/div[2]/div[2]/div[1]/div[1]/div[1]/span[1], (3) /html[1]/body[1]/div[1]/div[1]/main[1]/section[4]/div[1]/div[3]/div[2]/div[4]/div[1]/div[1]/div[1]/span[1]. These are the first span elements inside the inner-most div of the first, second, and fourth skill cards respectively. Remove them from the rendered output entirely so they no longer appear on the page.

new_feature_requests.summary: Hero section with bold headline 'Creative Design, Unique Ideas' and CTA button 'View My Work'
About Me section with bio text
Skills Gallery highlighting Adobe Photoshop, Illustrator, CorelDRAW, and InDesign with icons
Education Timeline from Nimatpur Girls High School to +3 Degree and PGDCA/Multimedia at Mayuri Multimedia
3-column masonry portfolio grid for logos, posters, and social media content
Contact section with form, email (sulekhasaho09865@gmail.com), and phone number
Sticky navigation with links to Home, Work, Education, and Contact
Service cards for Logo Design, Poster Design, and Social Media Management
Footer with address (Pasarbindha, Balasore) and social media placeholders
Update About Me section: remove '5+ years experience' and replace with '0 experience' (user is a fresher)
Admin Dashboard with secure login (Internet Identity) for Sulekha to upload, replace, and manage portfolio images directly without coding
Dynamic portfolio gallery that reflects changes made from the Admin panel in real time, with lightbox and category filters (Branding, Social Media, Print Media)

Create a Motoko backend actor that stores portfolio items (id, title, category, imageData as Blob/base64 text, order) in stable storage. Expose update functions: addPortfolioItem, replacePortfolioItem, deletePortfolioItem, and a query function getPortfolioItems. Guard all update calls with a principal-based admin check — only a hardcoded admin principal (set at deploy time) may call mutating functions.

In frontend/src/components/Skills.tsx, remove the three <span> elements identified by the following XPaths from the skill proficiency cards rendered in section 4: (1) /html[1]/body[1]/div[1]/div[1]/main[1]/section[4]/div[1]/div[3]/div[2]/div[1]/div[1]/div[1]/div[1]/span[1], (2) /html[1]/body[1]/div[1]/div[1]/main[1]/section[4]/div[1]/div[3]/div[2]/div[2]/div[1]/div[1]/div[1]/span[1], (3) /html[1]/body[1]/div[1]/div[1]/main[1]/section[4]/div[1]/div[3]/div[2]/div[4]/div[1]/div[1]/div[1]/span[1]. These are the first span elements inside the inner-most div of the first, second, and fourth skill cards respectively. Remove them from the rendered output entirely so they no longer appear on the page.
